rulings.Case Title: MARSAE SCOTT et al VS. LENNAR CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 30, 2011, line 13, DEFENDANT CH2M HILL MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO CREATE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO BOTH EXPOSURE TO ASBESTOS AND EXPOSURE TO "OTHER" TOXINS. MOREOVER, PLAINTIFF IS REQUIRED TO SPECIFICALLY DELINEATE IN ITS COMPLAINT THE SPECIFIC TOXIN ALLEGED TO HAVE CAUSED PLAINTIFFS' INJURIES. BOCKRATH V. ALDRICH CHEMICAL CO. (1999) 21 CAL.4TH 71, 80. PLAINTIFFS' ONLY REFER TO ASBESTOS DUST. FURTHER, THE DEFENDANTS OBJECTIONS TO PLAINTIFFS' EVIDENCE IN EXHIBIT 4, 6, 14A-J, ARE SUSTAINED ON THE BASIS OF LACK OF PERSONAL KNOWLEDGE AND HEARSAY. ALSO DEFENDANT BALL'S OBJECTION TO EXHIBITS 13, 14, 15, 16, 17 AND 18 ARE SUSTAINED AS IRRELEVANT GIVEN THE BOCKRATH DECISION. AS TO DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT BASED ON THE PUBLIC NUISANCE CAUSE OF ACTION, IT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PRESENT ANY EVIDENCE OF THE PRESENCE OF ASBESTOS LADEN DUST AND THAT THE LEVELS, IF ANY, WERE INJURIOUS TO HEALTH. THERE IS, THEREFORE, NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO CAUSATION. FINALLY, PLAINTIFFS' CAUSES OF ACTION FOR EMOTIONAL DISTRESS ALSO FAIL AND DEFENDANTS MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED. THESE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE BASED ON DEFENDANTS' ALLEGED DISREGARD OF HEALTH THREAT CREATED BY AIRBORNE ASBESTOS AND PLAINTIFFS' FEARS OF DEVELOPING CANCER AS A RESULT OF THE EXPOSURE. AS NOTED ABOVE, PLAINTIFFS' FAILED TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO "EXPOSURE" AND UNFOUNDED FEAR DOES NOT SUPPORT SUCH CLAIMS. POTTER V. FIRESTONE TIRE & RUBBER CO. (1993) 6 CAL.4TH 965, 1004. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: JUDY WILLIAMS VS. BLUE SHIELD OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 30, 2011, line 1, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PHYSICIAN'S SERVICE DBA BLUE SHIELD MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION (BREACH OF IMPLIED DUTY) IS GRANTED. AS PLAINTIFF'S TORT CLAIM IS BASED ON THE BREACH OF CONTRACT, THE ONLY ACT GIVING RISE TO "INJURY" IS THE MARCH 25, 2005 EOB LETTER CONFIRMING THE AMOUNT DEFENDANT WAS WILLING TO PAY OR NOT PAY ON PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM. THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS ON THIS CLAIM WAS IN 2007. CCP SECTION 17208. THEREFORE, THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS HAD PAST BEFORE PLAINTIFF FILED THIS SUIT. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE REMAINING CLAIMS BASED ON THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS IS DENIED. IT IS ALSO DENIED ON DEFENDANT'S ARGUMENT THAT PLAINTIFF LACKS STANDING BECAUSE HE SUFFERED NO MONEY DAMAGES. PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGED HARM IS HAVING TO PAY FOR SERVICES THEY DID NOT RECEIVE FROM DEFENDANT AND FOR BEING SUBJECT TO FEES FOR THE OUT-OF-NETWORK CARE WHILE PLAINTIFF WAS AT THE IN-NETWORK HOSPITAL. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS ALSO DENIED ON THE BASIS THAT PLAINTIFF LACKS STANDING TO REQUEST DECLARATORY AND INJUNCTIVE RELIEF. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: JUDY WILLIAMS VS. BLUE SHIELD OF CALIFORNIA et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 30, 2011, line 1, Plaintiff Judy Williams, On Behalf Of Themselves And Others, Russell Williams, On Behalf Of Themselves And Others' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And Demands For Documents. Plaintiff¿s Motion to Compel Responses to Request for Interrogatories is granted in part. The Motion to Compel Responses to Interrogatories 3 -6 is granted. The Requests for Response to Interrogs. 1 and 2 is denied. These exceed the scope of the class defined by the complaint. The Request for Production of Documents is granted. =(302/LMG)
